info.name: Reach TaskQ — Stress
info.description:
Knob-driven stress collection for reach-taskq. Each folder is one targeted load shape; every assertion is configurable via collection variables.

## Knobs (override via Newman `--env-var key=val` or environment file)

| Knob | Default | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `concurrencyN`        | 10  | parallel `pm.sendRequest` per request |
| `iterationOps`        | 50  | total ops per request (burst size) |
| `successPct`          | 70  | mix: success outcome |
| `flapPct`             | 20  | mix: flap (retry-then-succeed) |
| `failPct`             | 10  | mix: terminal fail |
| `idempotentPct`       | 30  | % of submits sent with `Idempotency-Key` |
| `idempotencyDupPct`   | 50  | of those keys, % that REUSE an existing key |
| `maxAttempts`         | 5   | per-job attempt budget |
| `payloadKb`           | 0   | extra padding bytes (KiB) injected into payload |
| `errorRateThreshold`  | 0.01| max acceptable failed-call ratio |
| `p95Threshold`        | 250 | max acceptable p95 submit latency (ms) |
| `saturateStartConc`   | 10  | escalating concurrency start |
| `saturateMaxConc`     | 100 | escalating concurrency cap |
| `saturateStep`        | 10  | escalating concurrency step |

All knobs map 1:1 to Newman flags (e.g. `--env-var concurrencyN=64`)

## Folders
1. Hammer · Submit (configurable mix)
2. Hammer · Idempotency replay storm
3. Hammer · Read-heavy (stats + lists)
4. Hammer · Get-by-id fan-out
5. Hammer · DLQ flood + replay storm
6. Hammer · Saturate (escalating concurrency)
7. Hammer · Payload size stress
8. Verify · Metrics summary (asserts against /actuator/prometheus)

item.name: 0 · Run all (one-click orchestrator)
item.description: Click Send on either request inside this folder. The orchestrator reads every knob from the collection Variables tab, runs all stress scenarios end-to-end, and prints a single consolidated report in the Postman test results console.

exec: function _p(xs, q) { if (!xs || !xs.length) return 0; const s=[...xs].sort((a,b)=>a-b); return s[Math.min(s.length-1, Math.floor(q*s.length))]; }
function _pickOutcome(sp, fp, xp) { const r=Math.random()*(sp+fp+xp); if (r<sp) return 'success'; if (r<sp+fp) return 'flap'; return 'fail'; }
// Echo config to console — useful for confirming what your changes look like.
console.log('▶ Backend says queue stats:', pm.response.text());
function _p(xs, q) { if (!xs || !xs.length) return 0; const s=[...xs].sort((a,b)=>a-b); return s[Math.min(s.length-1, Math.floor(q*s.length))]; }
function _pickOutcome(sp, fp, xp) { const r=Math.random()*(sp+fp+xp); if (r<sp) return 'success'; if (r<sp+fp) return 'flap'; return 'fail'; }
// One-click orchestrator. Click 'Send' to run every stress scenario in sequence
// using whatever config you set in the collection Variables tab.
// Final report is rendered by the post-response test script.
var report = { startedAt: new Date().toISOString(), cfg, scenarios: [] };
var totals = { req: 0, ok: 0, fail: 0, fivexx: 0, fourxx: 0, latencies: [] };
// --- helper: run N HTTP ops at concurrency C, with per-op factory; calls cb(stats) when done.
function runBurst(name, N, C, opFactory, cb, excludeFromTotals) {
var stats = { name, N, C, ok2xx:0, http200:0, http202:0, http409:0, http429:0, http5xx:0, other:0, latencies: [] };
var payload = { outcome: out, i, padding: _pad(cfg.payloadKb) };
return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST', header: headers,
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload, maxAttempts: out === 'fail' ? 1 : cfg.maxAttempts }) } };
function fIdemSame(key) { return function (i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST',
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'success', i } }) } }; }; }
function fIdemMismatch(key) { return function (i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST',
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'fail', i, drift: Math.random() } }) } }; }; }
function fStats(i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/queues/' + cfg.queue + '/stats', method: 'GET', header: { 'X-API-Key': cfg.apiKey } }; }
function fListJobs(i) { var fs=['','SUCCEEDED','DEAD','READY','SCHEDULED']; var f=fs[i % fs.length];
return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs?limit=20' + (f ? '&status=' + f : ''), method: 'GET', header: { 'X-API-Key': cfg.apiKey } }; }
function fGetById(id) { return function (i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs/' + id, method: 'GET', header: { 'X-API-Key': cfg.apiKey } }; }; }
function fFailSubmit(i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST', header: headersBase,
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'fail', i }, maxAttempts: 1 }) } }; }
function fSizeSubmit(kb) { var pad = 'x'.repeat(kb * 1024); return function (i) { return { url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST', header: headersBase,
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'success', padding: pad } }) } }; }; }
// --- chain scenarios. Each step receives next() to invoke the following step.
function step1(next) { runBurst('1 · Submit (mixed)', ops, conc, fSubmitMixed, function(){ next(); }); }
runBurst('2a · Idempotent same-key storm', ops, conc, fIdemSame(key), function(){ next(); });
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'success', seed: true } }) } },
function(){ runBurst('2b · Idempotent mismatch storm', ops, conc, fIdemMismatch(key), function(){ next(); }); });
function step4(next) { runBurst('3a · Read-heavy queue stats', ops, conc, fStats, function(){ next(); }); }
function step5(next) { runBurst('3b · Read-heavy list jobs',  ops, conc, fListJobs, function(){ next(); }); }
pm.sendRequest({ url: cfg.baseUrl + '/v1/jobs', method: 'POST', header: headersBase,
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'success' } }) } },
var id = (r && r.code === 202) ? (function(){ try { return r.json().id; } catch(_) { return null; } })() : null;
if (!id) { report.scenarios.push({ name: '4 · Get-by-id fan-out', skipped: 'no id from seed submit' }); next(); return; }
runBurst('4 · Get-by-id fan-out', ops, conc, fGetById(id), function(){ next(); });
// DLQ flood with smaller burst to keep total runtime reasonable.
runBurst('5 · DLQ flood', dlqOps, conc, fFailSubmit, function(){ next(); });
// Saturate: ramp from start to max in step increments, opsPerLevel small.
for (var c = cfg.saturateStartConc; c <= cfg.saturateMaxConc; c += cfg.saturateStep) levels.push(c);
report.scenarios.push({ name: '6 · Saturate ramp', levels: stepResults });
var C = levels[idx++], N = Math.max(20, Math.floor(ops / 2));
runBurst('6 · saturate@C=' + C, N, C, fSubmitMixed, function (s) {
stepResults.push({ C, N, ok: s.ok2xx, fivexx: s.http5xx, p95: s.p95, rps: s.rps });
if (s.http5xx > 0 || errRate > cfg.errorRateThreshold) { report.scenarios.push({ name: '6 · Saturate ramp', levels: stepResults, knee: { C, errRate, fivexx: s.http5xx } }); next(); }
if (sIdx >= sizes.length) { report.scenarios.push({ name: '7 · Payload size sweep', sizes: results }); next(); return; }
var kb = sizes[sIdx++], N = Math.max(5, Math.floor(ops / 4));
runBurst('7 · size@' + kb + 'KiB', N, conc, fSizeSubmit(kb), function (s) {
results.push({ kb, N, ok: s.ok2xx, p413: 0, fivexx: s.http5xx, p95: s.p95 });
pm.sendRequest({ url: cfg.baseUrl + '/actuator/prometheus', method: 'GET' }, function (_e, r) {

function valOf(name, lab) { var re = new RegExp('^' + name + (lab ? '\\{[^}]*' + lab + '[^}]*\\}' : '(?:\\{[^}]*\\})?') + '\\s+([0-9eE.+-]+)', 'm'); var m = text.match(re); return m ? parseFloat(m[1]) : null; }

function sumOf(name, lab) { var re = new RegExp('^' + name + '\\{[^}]*' + lab + '[^}]*\\}\\s+([0-9eE.+-]+)', 'gm'); var t = 0, m; while ((m = re.exec(text)) !== null) t += parseFloat(m[1]); return t; }
report.alerts = alerts.map(function (a) { return { name: a.labels.alertname, state: a.state, severity: a.labels.severity, summary: (a.annotations || {}).summary }; });
var steps = [step1, step2, step3, step4, step5, step6, step7, step8, step9, step10, step11];
function chain(i) { if (i >= steps.length) return done(); steps[i](function(){ chain(i + 1); }); }
pm.test('orchestrator finished', function () { pm.expect.fail('No report — orchestrator did not finish. Check pre-request console.'); });
console.log('  REACH TASKQ — Run-All Stress Report');
if (s.skipped) { console.log('  • ' + s.name + ' — SKIPPED (' + s.skipped + ')'); return; }
s.levels.forEach(function (l) { console.log('      C=' + String(l.C).padStart(3) + '  ok=' + l.ok + '/' + l.N + '  5xx=' + l.fivexx + '  p95=' + l.p95 + 'ms  rps=' + l.rps); });
if (s.knee) console.log('      >> KNEE at C=' + s.knee.C + '  errRate=' + s.knee.errRate.toFixed(4) + '  5xx=' + s.knee.fivexx);
s.sizes.forEach(function (z) { console.log('      ' + String(z.kb).padStart(3) + 'KiB  ok=' + z.ok + '/' + z.N + '  5xx=' + z.fivexx + '  p95=' + z.p95 + 'ms'); });
var line = '  • ' + s.name.padEnd(36) + ' N=' + String(s.N).padStart(4) + ' C=' + String(s.C).padStart(3);
line += '  p50=' + s.p50 + 'ms  p95=' + s.p95 + 'ms  p99=' + s.p99 + 'ms  rps=' + s.rps + '  wall=' + s.wallMs + 'ms';
console.log('Totals:    requests=' + rep.totals.requests + '  2xx=' + rep.totals.ok2xx + '  4xx=' + rep.totals.fourxx + '  5xx=' + rep.totals.fivexx + '  fail=' + rep.totals.fail);
console.log('Latency:   p50=' + rep.totals.p50 + 'ms  p95=' + rep.totals.p95 + 'ms  p99=' + rep.totals.p99 + 'ms');

console.log('Metrics:   liveThreads=' + rep.metrics.liveThreads + '  heap=' + (rep.metrics.heapPct*100).toFixed(1) + '%  hikariActive=' + rep.metrics.hikariActive + '  hikariPending=' + rep.metrics.hikariPending);
rep.alerts.forEach(function (a) { console.log('  ! ' + a.name + ' (' + a.state + ', severity=' + a.severity + ')'); });
pm.test('aggregate: 0 unexpected 5xx (system survives mixed load)', function () {
pm.test('aggregate: p95 < ' + aggP95 + 'ms (multi-shape, override aggregateP95Threshold)', function () {
var crit = rep.alerts.filter(function (a) { return a.severity === 'critical' && a.state === 'firing'; });
pm.test('no critical alerts firing', function () { pm.expect(crit, JSON.stringify(crit)).to.have.length(0); });

pm.sendRequest({ url: `${cfg.baseUrl}/v1/jobs`, method: 'POST', header: headers, body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify(body) } }, (err, res) => {

else if (res.code === 200) { stats.code200++; try { ids.add(res.json().id); } catch(_){} }

pm.test('all responses point at one id', () => pm.expect(r.uniqIds).to.eql(1));

item.name: Same key but mismatch payload (409 storm)

item.name: Step A · flood DLQ (fail jobs, maxAttempts=1)

exec: function _p(xs, q) { if (!xs || !xs.length) return 0; const s=[...xs].sort((a,b)=>a-b); return s[Math.min(s.length-1, Math.floor(q*s.length))]; }
function _pickOutcome(sp, fp, xp) { const r=Math.random()*(sp+fp+xp); if (r<sp) return 'success'; if (r<sp+fp) return 'flap'; return 'fail'; }
const N = cfg.iterationOps, C = Math.max(1, Math.min(cfg.concurrencyN, N));
let launched = 0, completed = 0, started = Date.now(), ok = 0, fail = 0;
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'fail', i }, maxAttempts: 1 }) }
if (!e && r && r.code === 202) { ok++; try { ids.push(r.json().id); } catch(_){} } else fail++;
if (completed === N) pm.collectionVariables.set('dlqFlood', JSON.stringify({ N, C, ok, fail, ids, wallMs: Date.now() - started }));

if (!N) { pm.collectionVariables.set('replayStorm', JSON.stringify({ N: 0, ok: 0, fail: 0, latencies: [] })); return; }
pm.sendRequest({ url: `${cfg.baseUrl}/v1/dlq/${id}/replay`, method: 'POST', header: { 'X-API-Key': cfg.apiKey } }, (e2, r2) => {
if (!e2 && r2 && r2.code === 200) stats.ok++; else stats.fail++;
if (completed === N) pm.collectionVariables.set('replayStorm', JSON.stringify({ ...stats, wallMs: Date.now() - started, N, C }));

item.name: Ramp concurrency until error budget exceeded

exec: function _p(xs, q) { if (!xs || !xs.length) return 0; const s=[...xs].sort((a,b)=>a-b); return s[Math.min(s.length-1, Math.floor(q*s.length))]; }
function _pickOutcome(sp, fp, xp) { const r=Math.random()*(sp+fp+xp); if (r<sp) return 'success'; if (r<sp+fp) return 'flap'; return 'fail'; }
for (let c = cfg.saturateStartConc; c <= cfg.saturateMaxConc; c += cfg.saturateStep) levels.push(c);
let launched = 0, completed = 0, started = Date.now(), ok = 0, fail = 0, fivexx = 0;
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ queue: cfg.queue, type: 'echo', payload: { outcome: 'success', i } }) }
if (!e && r && r.code >= 200 && r.code < 300) ok++; else fail++;
const rps = N * 1000 / Math.max(1, Date.now() - started);
results.push({ C, N, ok, fail, fivexx, p95, rps: Math.round(rps), wall: Date.now() - started });
function finalize() { pm.collectionVariables.set('saturate', JSON.stringify({ levels: results, knee: results[results.length - 1] })); }
function _p(xs, q) { if (!xs || !xs.length) return 0; const s=[...xs].sort((a,b)=>a-b); return s[Math.min(s.length-1, Math.floor(q*s.length))]; }
function _pickOutcome(sp, fp, xp) { const r=Math.random()*(sp+fp+xp); if (r<sp) return 'success'; if (r<sp+fp) return 'flap'; return 'fail'; }
pm.test('reached at least 1 successful level', () => pm.expect((r.levels || []).length).to.be.at.least(1));
